title: What are some common methods provided by the Thread class?

answers.junior: Common methods: start(), run(), join(), sleep(), interrupt().
answers.middle: start() begins execution, run() contains logic, join() waits for completion, sleep() pauses, interrupt() signals stop. Also setPriority() and setDaemon().
answers.senior: Thread class offers methods for control: start(), run(), join(), sleep(), interrupt(), setPriority(), setDaemon(). Avoid deprecated methods like stop(), suspend(), resume(). Handle InterruptedException properly.
answers.tricky: Use higher-level concurrency utilities for robust thread management. Deprecated methods can cause inconsistent states. Always handle exceptions and monitor thread health in production systems.
